                                                               CALENDAR OF FEATURES
                                          In addition to the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and
                                          Earnings, special features appear in most of the issues, as shown below:

                                                                  Jan.   Feb.   Mar.   Apr.   May   June   July   Aug.   Sept.    Oct.   Nov.
                     Household data
                  Annual averages                                  X
                  Revised seasonally adjusted series                      X
                  Quarterly averages:
                   Seasonally adjusted data
                   Persons not in labor force
                   Persons of Spanish origin                       X                    x                   X                       X
                   Vietnam-era veterans and nonveterans
                     Establishment data
                  National annual averages:
                   Industry divisions (preliminary)                X
                   Industry detail (final)                                       X
                  Women employment (national)                             X                    X                   X                      X
                  National data adjusted t o new benchmarks                                                                       (1)
                  Revised seasonally adjusted series                                                (2)
                  State and area annual averages                                               X
                  Area definitions                                                             X

                       The issue that introduces the establishment data adjusted to new benchmarks varies. The October 1972 issue/
                 marks the introduction of March 1971 benchmarks.
                         Revised data introduced in June 1973.

                 tAs noted on page 7 of the January 1972 issue of this magazine, the series does not include earned pay withheld as a consequence of delays caused by the wage freeze in effect between August 15
              and November 14, 1971, and by administrative procedures of the Pay Board. These delays resulted from compliance with the Government's Economic Stabilization Program. Because these withheld
              payments were substantial and fell due under a significant number of new labor-management contracts in that industry.they affected a large proportion of SIC 372. The series including these pay
              ments for September 1971 - March 1973 are shown below.
                 A 17 -cent delayed payment for employees in a number of large companies was pending as a consequence of litigation in the courts arising out of the Government's Economic Stabilization Pro-
              gram. The issue is undergoing further litigation. These data will later be revised, if necessary, to include this additional delayed payment:

                      BLS Handbook of Methods for Surveys and Studies—Chapter 25. Output Per Man-Hour Measures, Private Economy.
                           2
                              "Annual rate" refers to total man-hours for 1 week in the month, seasonally adjusted, and expressed as an annual equivalent.
                           3
                              Percent change compounded at annual rates.
                           p-preliminary.      M Q T E : Dttailt may not add to totals btcaust of rounding.

                           SOURCE: Bureau of Labor Statistics, Office of Productivity and Technology.

                    Wages and salaries of employees plus employers' contributions for social insurance and private benefit plans. Data also include an estimate of wages, salaries, and supplemental payments for
              the self-employed, except for nonfinancial corporations where there are no self-employed.

                    Nonlabor payments include profits, depreciation, interest, rental income, and indirect taxes.
                    Current dollar gross product divided by constant dollar gross product.
                    Quarterly measures adjusted to annual estimates of output (gross product originating) from the Bureau of Economic Analysis, U.S. Department of Commerce.

                    Includes depreciation, interest, and indirect taxes.
                    Includes corporate profits before taxes and inventory valuation adjustment.

            CONCEPTS                                                                       The civilian labor force comprises the total of all civilians
                                                                                      classified as employed or unemployed in accordance with the
               Employed persons comprise (a) all those who during the                 criteria described above. The "total labor force" also includes
           survey week did any work at all as paid employees, in their own            members of the Armed Forces stationed either in the United
           business, profession, or farm, or who worked 15 hours or more              States or abroad.
           as unpaid workers in an enterprise operated by a member of the                  The unemployment rate represents the number unemployed
           family, and (b) all those who were not working but who had jobs            as a percent of the civilian labor force. This measure can also be
           or businesses from which they were temporarily absent because              computed for groups within the labor force classified by sex,
           of illness, bad weather, vacation, labor-management dispute, or            age, marital status, color, etc. The job-loser, job-leaver, reentrant,
           personal reasons, whether or not they were paid by their                   and new entrant rates are each calculated as a percent of the
           employers for the time off, and whether or not they were                   civilian labor force; the sum of the rates for the four groups thus
           seeking other jobs.                                                        equals the total unemployment rate.

            COLLECTION                                                                All national, State, and area employment, hours, earnings,
                                                                                   and labor turnover series are classified in accordance with the
                Payroll reports provide current information on wage and            Standard Industrial Classification Manual, Bureau of the Budget,

            nonagricultural establishments, by industry and geographic

            Federal-State cooperation                                              refer to persons on establishment payrolls who received pay for
                                                                                   any part of the pay period which includes the 12th of the
                Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the            month. For Federal Government establishments, employment
